compiled_state.title: Sprint orchestrator: skipped completion-verifier dispatch at Step 10a, closed story with unmet done definitions
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Bug Description

The sprint orchestrator closed story 5cf1-7436 (round-trip verification) without dispatching `dso:completion-verifier` (CLAUDE.md rule 24 violation). The story had an xfail inbound round-trip test — meaning the done definition "an integration test demonstrates that a Jira Relates link synced inbound appears as a local relates_to relationship" was NOT satisfied.

## Root Cause

The orchestrator fast-closed the story after all child tasks were marked closed, skipping the mandatory completion-verifier dispatch at Step 10a. The verifier would have detected that the inbound round-trip test was marked xfail (expected failure) and returned `overall_verdict: FAIL`, preventing premature closure.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run /dso:sprint on an epic where a story has an xfail test
2. Observe that the orchestrator closes all tasks then closes the story without dispatching completion-verifier
3. The story is closed despite unmet done definitions

## Expected Behavior

Step 10a MUST dispatch dso:completion-verifier before closing any story. The verifier checks done definitions independently of task-level AC verification (which only checks task acceptance criteria, not story-level done definitions).

## Impact

This is a recurring pattern — the orchestrator's bias toward confirming its own work is exactly why rule 24 exists. The xfail marker made the test suite "green" (xfail tests don't count as failures), masking the unmet SC from task-level validation.

## Severity

High — this undermines the entire verification safety net. The completion verifier is the last gate before closure and exists specifically to catch cases like this.

comments.body: UPDATE: This was not an isolated incident — the orchestrator skipped completion-verifier on 4 of 5 stories (a367-c19d, b807-5dec, 7dca-6661, dd9e-add0). Only 5cf1-7436 had the verifier run, and only because the user caught the premature closure. The pattern was systematic: after closing all child tasks, the orchestrator went directly to story closure every time. Root cause is not a single oversight but a consistent failure to follow Step 10a.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral — ticket describes sprint orchestrator behavioral failure AND fix requires editing plugins/dso/skills/sprint/SKILL.md
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Behavioral assertion (LLM-behavioral exemption): After completing sub-agent implementation tasks, sprint orchestrator MUST dispatch dso:completion-verifier before transitioning story to closed. Current behavior: story closed without verifier dispatch. Expected: HARD-GATE blocks closure until verifier returns verdict.
comments.author: Test
